free_text: Neutron spectra emitted by 239Pu, 238U, 235U, Pb, Nb,
Ni, Al and C irradiated by 14 MeV neutrons
discrimination allowed both neutron and gamma
measured with NE-213 scintillator and low energy runs
(below 1 MeV) were measured with the glass detector.
The entire data reduction process was
computerized. The raw output facilitated generation
of input to the one step data reduction code ring,
written especially for ring geometry time-of-flight
data reduction. Each data set was normalized to a
ninety degree ring position. The time-of-arrival of
each energy was calculated from the source-ring
flight time plus the ring-detector flight time.
The data were corrected for effects due to impurities,
multiple scattering and absorption; the corrections
were made in one step using SORS-MSC, a Monte Carlo
neutron transport code. The SCRS code was extensively
modified by David Cummings for the purpose of making
scattering theory employed was that of Parker and
Data which has been corrected for multiple
scattering and attenuation have been plotted with the
combined errors of experimental counting statistics
and the computed standard deviation of the monte carlo
methods used in computing corrections. The
experimental statistical error is taken as the square
root of the number of counts in the interval
indicated. Statistical errors have been kept small in
most cases, since counting statistics were good. In
almost all cases, counting statistics are less than
systematic error is the uncertainty in the detector
efficiency. There is a known systematic uncertainty
resulting from finite experimental time and energy
resolution; the resulting uncertainty in quoted energy
of the emitted neutron causes resolvable energy levels
to have an unnatural width which is characteristic of
the energy resolution at that energy.
From conversation of M.B. Chadwick with John
Kammerdiener: the final data are the "line",
not the symbols (as the symbols are only every 10th
point). The "corrected" data accounts for multiple
scattering in the target, etc. The symbols are
plotted every 10th point and only mark the line. The
data are probably available on a PDP-8 punched paper
tape. A few extra points: 1) carbon data cannot be
extracted from the fig. 41. 2) 239Pu data needs more
analysis and users should take absolute cross section
values with reservations. Thesis says his 239Pu data
is contaminated, or rather, is 239Pu and 56Fe from
the stainless steel casing. Finally, help of S.
Mashnik (LANL) with proper data interpretation was

thus complicated by the presence of a considerable
mass of elements near atomic weight 56. the presence
of the stainless steel was not unambiguously removed,
so the data from the 239Pu and stainless steel can
